channel.title: Manish Karki — writing
channel.description: Essays on AI engineering, RAG, evals, and the small craft of shipping software.

item.title: The RAG mistake I made three times before I caught it
item.description: Chunking by tokens, not by meaning. Retrieved snippets that ended mid-sentence kept poisoning the answer. Three fixes that took retrieval recall from 0.71 to 0.89.

item.title: WordPress was an apprenticeship in software
item.description: Five years of WordPress sites for non-technical clients taught me more about shipping software than any framework has since. A reluctant defence of the most unfashionable platform on the internet.

item.title: Eval-driven prompting, or: how I stopped vibe-coding LLMs
item.description: Without a test set, you're not iterating on a prompt — you're rearranging deck chairs. A small case for taking prompt evaluation as seriously as unit testing.
